subject: Lab machine with roll feeder — quick catch-up
body:
Hi Annalisa,

Hope things are good on your side in Rome — and that the thermoforming world's been kind to you since you got in touch.

I wanted to pick up the thread. When you'd asked about a lab machine with roll feeder, the right fit on our side is our ATF (or PF1-R) line — roll-fed thin sheet (0.2–1.5 mm), in a size that fits your lab space and throughput. Forming area and depth we can tailor; typical lab builds we've done in Europe sit in the roughly €90k–120k EXW range depending on size and options — I'd be happy to send a formal quote once we lock the spec. Lead time is usually in the 4–5 month range plus shipping. And we're actually shipping a lab machine to Italy very soon, so the timing's a good one to chat. If you'd like to see the kind of setup we're talking about, here's a short video: https://youtu.be/dbx5ZeU4wWs

A few things I'm curious about. What kind of parts or thicknesses are you looking to run on the roll-fed side — and is this for research, prototyping, or small-batch production? On raw materials: we're seeing a fair bit of resin and oil-related pressure from our side in India; has any of that been on your radar in Europe, or shifted how you're thinking about timing for new kit? And are you looking for any tooling or mould support alongside the machine, or more on the equipment side only? Some of the folks we talk to are also looking at energy-efficient or Industry 4.0-ready kit to tap into incentives — wondering if that's in the picture for you.

On our side we've been busy: we've got a lab machine going to Italy very soon (so you're in good company), and we've got references in the Netherlands (near Den Haag — Dezet and Dutch Tides) where you can see machines if that's ever useful.

No pressure at all — just wanted to say hello and put a number and a shape to the conversation. If a short web call sometime next week works better, I'm happy to do 15–20 minutes whenever suits you; just say the day.

They say the only constant in thermoforming is change — and the coffee consumption.

Rushabh
